description_long: This is a single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined fuselage and a distinctive yellow nose and tail section. The wings are straight with slight dihedral and display prominent black crosses and white markings, indicative of a World War II-era German aircraft. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned forward on the fuselage, featuring a clear canopy. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a tailwheel landing gear configuration. The landing gear is fixed in the down position, with two main wheels and a small tailwheel visible. The surface finish appears matte with visible panel lines and insignia, and the propeller has yellow tips.
description: A yellow-nosed, low-wing monoplane with a single propeller, enclosed cockpit, and WWII-era German markings. Features fixed landing gear and a conventional tail design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the yellow nose, three-blade propeller, and wide wingspan with visible landing gear.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cockpit canopy, wing markings, and tail section with black cross insignia.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, straight wing configuration, and all surface markings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the tailwheel, fuselage number, and additional wing and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings and slight dihedral; no winglets or variable geometry.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
